title: Charred Polish Fish Gyoza
description: A charred, molten, solo-dinner polish dish featuring thai fish sauce, en-croute, keto-friendly. This recipe makes 4-6 gyoza and serves 1 person as a main course.
instructions: Heat 1/4 cup vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the Polish-style fish balls and sear until browned on all sides, about 3 minutes per side.
Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix together the Thai fish sauce, lime juice, chopped scallions, chopped cilantro, grated ginger, garlic powder, paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Place a wonton wrapper in the skillet and brush the edges with water.
Place 1/6 of the fish ball mixture in the center of the wrapper and fold the wrapper into a triangle, pressing the edges to seal.
Repeat with the remaining wrappers and filling, arranging the gyoza in the skillet with the seal-side down.
Cook for 2-3 minutes, until the bottoms of the gyoza are golden brown.
Flip the gyoza over and cook for an additional 2-3 minutes, until the tops are golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Serve immediately, garnished with chopped scallions and cilantro if desired.
To char the gyoza, place them under the broiler for 1-2 minutes, until the tops are slightly charred and bubbly.
Be careful not to burn the gyoza, as they can go from perfectly cooked to burnt quickly.
If desired, serve with a side of steamed vegetables or a salad for a more filling meal.
Alternatively, you can also serve the gyoza as a snack or appetizer, depending on your preference.
